issues.title: Benchmark harness measuring framework success
issues.description: Build a repeatable benchmark that runs a defined reference project (spec'd in advance) through agent execution and measures whether this framework earns its overhead. Three measures, per Brooks: (1) total token consumption, (2) % of project completed to spec, (3) wall-clock time. Design should compare scaffolded (to-execution Instance) vs unscaffolded baseline runs on the same spec. Needs its own grill → PRD before implementation.
acceptance_criteria: A reference spec exists against which % completion is mechanically assessable
One command (or documented procedure) produces all three measures for a run
Scaffolded vs unscaffolded comparison is possible on the same spec
issues.notes: Filed by Team Lead from Brooks's directive 2026-06-07, mid-EXEC-001 grill.

issues.title: Verify project-scoped publish auth bypasses 2FA on the next real release
issues.description: On the next real to-execution publish, confirm the RELEASE.md recipe publishes unattended (no OTP prompt / no E403). npm-personal-token may be a classic token that 2FA-gates writes; whoami can't prove write-2FA. If it OTP/E403s, overwrite the Keychain item (service npm-personal-token, account $USER) value with an automation/granular-automation token that bypasses 2FA — src/.npmrc and the recipe stay unchanged.
issues.notes: Filed 2026-06-09 as the deferred 2FA-bypass-on-write proof for EXEC-034 (Brooks).

issues.title: Ship the viewer server as a separate globally-installed package; sessions detect or connect to it
issues.description: Once EXEC-054 validates the in-repo auto-run server, extract it into its own package installed globally (separate from the to-execution Scaffolder package). Individual execution repos/sessions check whether the global server exists and connect to it instead of each running their own — one server, many Instances. Same hard requirements: lightweight, fast-running, zero/near-zero dependencies. Needs design decisions (port/discovery convention, multi-repo serving, version skew between server and Instances) — grill before implementation.
issues.notes: Filed 2026-06-10 (Brooks). Step 2 of the viewer-server track; acceptance criteria set after EXEC-054 validates and a grill resolves discovery/packaging. Re-identified from EXEC-047 on 2026-06-10: the original id collided with a PRD-007 hook slice filed in a parallel session; sprint-4 close gate caught the lineage mismatch.

issues.title: Edit and add issues from the status-page viewer
issues.description: Extend the status-page viewer from read-only to read-write for issues: add new issues and edit existing ones from the web page. Writes go through the server (EXEC-054/055) since a static page cannot write the .excn JSON; every write must remain schema-valid against issue.schema.json and respect the issue partitioning (backlog vs sprint partitions, scribe ownership of moves). Hard requirements: keep it as lightweight and fast as possible — no framework, minimal surface.
issues.notes: Filed 2026-06-10 (Brooks). Builds on the viewer-server track; interaction with scribe ownership of issue JSON to be resolved at grill time. Re-identified from EXEC-048 on 2026-06-10: the original id collided with a PRD-007 hook slice filed in a parallel session; sprint-4 close gate caught the lineage mismatch.
